scope: Docs-backed public block contract for AI-driven EDS page generation. Prefer sidekick-documented authoring patterns and examples where available.

components.name: Accordion
components.description: Each direct child row becomes one accordion item with a header cell and a content cell.
usageNotes: The JS contract for first-open and action-only-on-header is explicit.
Content cells are normalized to contain at least one paragraph.
ambiguityNotes: Some additional variants are styling-only and inferred from CSS selectors.

components.name: Ai Skills Checker
components.description: This component checks the safety of some clawhub links. It should be split into 4 rows. In the first row there should be the content itself and the safe/danger images. In the second row there should be the status messages.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary authored content. Follow the sidekick block page for the exact table/content shape and examples.

usageNotes: In the third row there should be the titles-change. In the fourth row will be the buttons.
ambiguityNotes: This entry was expanded from the sidekick block docs and local source file path, but the exact authoring table/row structure should still be taken from the documented examples when generating DOCX content.

components.name: Awards
components.description: This block displays an awards component.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Author this block using the repeatable item structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: awards-component --- this is required in order for the block to work properly
ambiguityNotes: This entry was expanded from the sidekick block docs and local source file path, but the exact authoring table/row structure should still be taken from the documented examples when generating DOCX content.

components.name: Awards Search
components.description: This block displays a search bar where you can search for awards and a dropdown menu where you can filter those awards by the year when they were received. It also generates the search items and displays them.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Author this block using the repeatable item structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: Note: this component might not work in sidekick or certain testing environments.
You will need to use this block in combination with the accordion block which will display the dropdown menu. You should add the class action-only-on-header to the accordion block.
Further metada is not needed, but you can still add it for various use cases you might desire in pages.(ex: add an id for a button to direct you to this component).
ambiguityNotes: This entry was expanded from the sidekick block docs and local source file path, but the exact authoring table/row structure should still be taken from the documented examples when generating DOCX content.

components.name: Barchart
components.description: Displays two elements which contain a barchart. Each element contains an image , a title , a list (which is the barchart) and some text.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: Author this block using the row/column table structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: The title is a h3 element.
Every 2 list elements will be displayed together in one line, one being the product name, the other its performance.
Each barchart will have it’s own table line with 2 columns one being the image the other being the chart and the rest of the content.
This block doesn’t support any other styles or functionality.
ambiguityNotes: This entry was expanded from the sidekick block docs and local source file path, but the exact authoring table/row structure should still be taken from the documented examples when generating DOCX content.

components.name: Cards
components.description: Each source row becomes one card. The block detects picture-only cells as image cells and all other cells as body content.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Repeatable cards.

usageNotes: The code is explicit about the image/body split and is safe for generation.
Generated card body content should remain simple rich text.

components.name: Columns
components.description: Rows of adjacent columns containing text and optional images. The block infers image columns automatically from picture-only cells.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: Each row contains one or more columns.

columns.description: Column cells rendered side by side.

usageNotes: Only the plain content-driven row/column contract is included here.
The implementation reads many section dataset values for carousels, dynamic links, tabs, store context, and background images. Those features are intentionally omitted.

Documented sample names: 3 columns - awards, 3 columns – 2 rows - benefits, 2 columns – video presentation, 3 columns - images, 1 column – with background image - v1, 1 column – with background image - v2.
ambiguityNotes: Columns is widely theme-customized in template CSS. Use this manifest entry only for plain rows and columns, not campaign-specific enhancements.

components.name: Columns Grid
components.description: This block displays a grid layout with flipping cards. The front of the card will be separated from the back by inserting a ‘---’ (<hr> element) inside every table cell. The cards support 4 sizes and grow relatively to the other cards. You add the size by inserting a p element with this text content at the top of every card: size-number-card. (number: 1 trough 4).

fields.name: rows
fields.description: Author this block using the row/column table structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

The first text, besides the icons, is the title, and must be an H3 tag. The second text is the text under the title. The rest are the list of features: Features title ----> must be written in italics Feature text ---> richtext zone

components.name: Form
components.description: This component will only render the fields by itself. For creating a new form you will need to create an excel file which will contain the fields, for each field its input type, a label(representing each field’s content),a default column which will render placeholders, a required column containing true for required attributes, a value column for which will contain either links to a file or in the buttons case its text and and if there is the case the file which will handle the submission with its source . A form can be inserted into the page like any block and it will only contain the link to the form’s data.

components.name: Hero
components.description: Content-first hero block. The code promotes a picture ahead of the text when both are present and supports plain rich text plus button content.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary hero content. Real code looks for headings, paragraphs, lists, buttons, and an optional picture.

fields.name: image
fields.description: Optional hero picture. If a picture exists before the main heading in the source markup, the decorate code moves it into a dedicated hero picture area.

usageNotes: The repository also supports many section dataset customizations, OS-specific links, and store-driven discount elements, but those are intentionally excluded from this first contract.
Documented sample names: Hero, Hero with black background, Hero black background, non black-friday, Hero with 2 buttons and dynamic price.
ambiguityNotes: Hero variants are mostly inferred from CSS selectors rather than a dedicated authoring spec.
Do not generate section dataset fields such as app-store mappings or modal-refresh flags unless later documentation confirms them.

sectionMetadata.name: Discount

sectionMetadata.name: Label
sectionMetadata.description: Shows the text under the discount percentage in the bubble.

sectionMetadata.name: TextColor
sectionMetadata.description: Primary color of the text. Only white is available for this metadata.

sectionMetadata.name: BackgroundColor
sectionMetadata.description: Primary color of the background. Only black is available. Percent-product functionality: If you need to use store text variables inside some text in order to display dynamic prices or discounts include the product code and variation in this metadata.

components.name: Image Columns
components.description: Row-based block where each cell is treated either as an image column or a text column. The implementation can also replace a text cell with a local MP4 video in some themed cases.
usageNotes: Generate the plain image-plus-text structure first.
The MP4 substitution path is repo-specific and depends on section classes or local file naming; it is not part of the safe first contract.
Documented sample names: Image to the left, Image to the right.
ambiguityNotes: Several visual variants are inferred from CSS only.
Theme-specific section classes such as columnvideo are intentionally omitted from the contract.

components.name: Link Checker
components.description: This component displays a block that checks links. It should have 5 rows. The first row should contain the two images (for safe/unsafe link) followed by a some text a title(h1) and a description.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary authored content. Follow the sidekick block page for the exact table/content shape and examples.

usageNotes: The second row should contain the <status-messages> tag (Safe, default-good, default). The third row should contain the text displayed under the input field.
The fourth row should contain the <titles-change> tag (Safe ,So_far_so_good_1 ,So_far_so_good_2 ,Default)
The fifth row should contain the <buttons> tag and the buttons.

components.name: New Product Boxes
components.description: Table-driven pricing cards for consumer products. The block derives live pricing, discounts, and buy links from store product identifiers configured at section level, while most visible card content is authored in ordered table rows.

fields.name: cards
fields.description: One authored table per product card.

greenTag.description: Optional top tag such as 'Most Popular'. The special value 'demo-box' is also checked by the implementation.

title.description: Primary product title row. Usually contains a linked product name.

blueTags.description: Optional row containing zero or more blue pill/tag fragments.

subtitle.description: Primary descriptive subtitle shown above pricing.

planOptions.description: Optional row used as radio-label source for yearly/monthly/third-button plan switching. The implementation reads labels from child nodes and supports a '[checked]' marker.

perPrice.description: Optional price suffix row. The implementation strips a literal '0' placeholder and appends the remainder as superscript text.

billedText.description: Billed/tax messaging associated with the current plan.

buyLinks.description: Primary buy CTA and optional secondary link/button row.

underBuyLink.description: Optional text or popup trigger below the buy button.

benefits.description: Feature tables that are converted into benefits lists. The code supports markers such as '[checkmark]', '[add-on]', '->', '?pill', and add-on price placeholders.

addOnBilledText.description: Optional billed text for add-on pricing.

subtitle2.description: Optional secondary subtitle shown below the first divider.

sectionMetadata.name: products
sectionMetadata.description: Mandatory EDS section metadata. These products are shown when the component first loads. After Franklin processing this is exposed to the block as data-products / section.dataset.products.

sectionMetadata.name: family-products
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for the family tab product list. Same tuple format as products.

sectionMetadata.name: monthly-products
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for the second radio button. Mandatory when the card has 2 radio buttons. Despite the name, the second radio can point to any product tuple, not only monthly plans.

sectionMetadata.name: third-radio-button-products
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for the third radio button. Mandatory when the card has 3 radio buttons.

sectionMetadata.name: add-on-products
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for products used in the add-on section.

sectionMetadata.name: add-on-monthly-products
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for second-radio products in the add-on section. The implementation also accepts a '0' placeholder for cards without a second add-on option.

sectionMetadata.name: id
sectionMetadata.description: Optional EDS section metadata key that overrides the default component id.

sectionMetadata.name: add-on-product-name
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for the title displayed in the add-on section.

sectionMetadata.name: save-text
sectionMetadata.description: EDS section metadata for the static label shown before the computed discount percentage, for example 'Save'.

usageNotes: This block is required for pricing previews, but generated content should stay close to the authored row order used by the implementation.
Visible pricing numbers are not authored directly. They are derived from section metadata that Franklin exposes as section.dataset values and from store data attributes added during decoration.
The tuple format used by pricing metadata is 'wanted product / number of devices / number of years', for example 'us_i_m/5/1'.
If a card exposes 2 radio buttons, 'monthly-products' is mandatory. If it exposes 3 radio buttons, 'third-radio-button-products' is mandatory.
For pricing generation, another agent should emit the section-level EDS metadata and the authored card rows together.
Documented sample names: New-prod-boxes (with family slider), New prod boxes (dynamic under_price_text).
ambiguityNotes: The main pricing lists and labels are now explicit, but repo-only settings such as 'type' and 'hide-decimals' still come primarily from code rather than author documentation.

The block contains advanced add-on, family/individual toggle, and synchronized plan-switch behavior. Those behaviors are code-backed but should be treated as optional extensions until dedicated generation docs exist.

components.name: Password Generator
components.description: This component generates a password and allows you to copy it to your clipboard. It also supports some password conditions that you will have to check.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary authored content. Follow the sidekick block page for the exact table/content shape and examples.

usageNotes: Content wise the block is split into three rows. One first row should contain a title (h1 element). One row will be split into two columns. The first column will contain the generator part and the other will contain the copy button (link of #copy-password ). One row should contain the privacy policy. Component specific tags:
<password-generator> - this tag should be put in the first row of any column
strong-weak-text - you can insert this text in order to flag where the strenght of the generated password will be displayed followed by 4 texts split by a comma
<privacy-policy> - flags the row that contains the privacy policy

components.name: Quote
components.description: Transforms each item into a quote card with a star rating, quote body, and optional author.

stars.name: stars
stars.description: Asterisks-only source text, typically in emphasis markup, because the code counts literal '*' characters.

author.description: Author line, typically authored in strong markup.

usageNotes: Because the implementation accesses the stars element directly, the star line should be treated as required in generated content.
This block appears to be styled mainly within hero contexts.

components.name: Tabs
components.description: This functionality represents what can be described as a "component within a component."

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary authored content. Follow the sidekick block page for the exact table/content shape and examples.

usageNotes: The tabs component is designed to showcase various components across different tabs. For instance, in the first example, it is integrated with the accordion component to create a "section requirement" section.
Documented sample names: Tabs with accordion, Tabs with various components.
ambiguityNotes: This entry was expanded from the sidekick block docs and local source file path, but the exact authoring table/row structure should still be taken from the documented examples when generating DOCX content.

components.name: Tabs Component
components.description: This component will display a tab menu. The first two rows should be the title and subtitle. Use bold font for the title. The third row will be the tab buttons. Each tab button should have its own column. The content for each slide will have its own table row and consist of 2 columns: -image -h3 title and text

fields.name: rows
fields.description: Author this block using the row/column table structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: Documented sample names: Tabs-component, Tabs-component blue headings.

components.name: Teaser Logos
components.description: Wraps each child item in a logo column. Best suited for a small set of logos or badges.

content.description: Usually an image, but the code will preserve arbitrary markup.

usageNotes: Prefer image-only items for predictable output.

components.name: Timeline Carousel
components.description: This block displays a carousel with an axis in the middle. Note: you can only add more items by adding more years on the main axis, or the additional slides won’t render.

fields.name: items
fields.description: Author this block using the repeatable item structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: -timeline-years: creates a cell for each year in the carousel
Documented sample names: With start position at the end.

components.name: Trusted Hero
components.description: This block displays a banner with a video. It supports h1 and h2 elements, normal text and buttons. In order for the block do be displayed properly the page should have a template of trusted.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Primary authored content. Follow the sidekick block page for the exact table/content shape and examples.

components.name: Trusted Main Carousel
components.description: Each slide will be split into two columns and will have its own row: first one the information and the background image. The left columns must contain an h2 element underlined as this will become the button in the carousel menu. Other supported elements are h3, buttons, images and normal text.

components.name: Trusted Teaser Impact
components.description: This block displays a ttile and some boxes. The title will be the first row in your word table, and each content box will have its own row.

fields.name: rows
fields.description: Author this block using the row/column table structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: The title box should contain a underlined text and a h2 element. Each content box should contain an icon and 2 other elements. The icon should not be the 2 nd element.
Note: in order for the first element of the title to be blue you should have a page metadata of template: trusted

components.name: Video
components.description: Supports YouTube, Vimeo, and direct MP4 links. An optional picture acts as a click-to-play placeholder.

fields.name: link
fields.description: Video source URL.

fields.name: placeholder
fields.description: Optional picture used as a click-to-play placeholder.

usageNotes: The visible source contract is simple and generation-safe.
Desktop alignment is read from section dataset, not block content. Keep it optional in downstream tooling.
ambiguityNotes: The code comment says 'left / middle / right' while the actual enum uses left, center, right. Use center, because that is what the code maps.

components.name: Video Carousel
components.description: This block displays a carousel with videos. Each video should have its own metadata properties. Metadata properties: -video{index}: this property should contain the embedded link to the video

fields.name: items
fields.description: Author this block using the repeatable item structure documented in the sidekick block page and its examples.

usageNotes: -title{index}: this property should contain the video title
